title: thymus chamaedrys subsp. comosus
intro: Thymus chamaedrys subsp. comosus, commonly known as the woolly thyme or creeping thyme, is a subspecies of the thyme plant that is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can be attributed to its essential oils, particularly thymol and carvacrol. The medicinal properties of woolly thyme have been found to provide health benefits such as reducing stress, improving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Woolly thyme can be prepared through herbal teas, infusions, and salves, making it a versatile and accessible remedy for various health concerns.
